title: centaurea singarensis
intro: Centaurea singarensis, also known as the Singareni knapweed, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where its roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Centaurea singarensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Centaurea singarensis typically involve drying the roots and leaves, which are then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
